user_message: Working on Adatum Corporation's executive brief -- need you to assess the risk.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 11:34 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with executive brief. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 13:49 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for executive brief. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 10:28 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Adatum Corporation executive brief

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-C trap (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-C failure mode (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-C -- Skips the 'waiting on whom' question
